info.title: Tellus Open Platform API
info.summary: Charger-platform integration API for CPMS, EMS, aggregators, and chargers.
info.description:
The Tellus Open Platform API enables Charging Point Management Systems (CPMS),
Energy Management Systems (EMS), aggregators and chargers themselves to integrate
with the Tellus charging-platform infrastructure.

The API is divided into three logical surfaces:

- **Operator-side API** (`/v1/operator/...`) — used by CPMS, EMS, and aggregators
  to query the fleet, retrieve charging records, control chargers, schedule charging,
  issue load-flexibility commands, and subscribe to a real-time telemetry WebSocket.
- **Charger-side API** (`/v1/device/...`) — used by chargers themselves for
  registration, heartbeat, telemetry, event reporting, charging-record upload,
  command fetching (long-polling), command-status reporting, and firmware-upgrade
  status.
- **Operator-to-operator interop (legacy)** — an older roaming-style interface
  (PascalCase, PUT-based) used for data exchange between operator platforms,
  modelled after China's national charging-interop standard. Most modern partners
  should integrate via the Operator-side API instead.

All requests use HTTPS (TLS 1.2 or higher). Plain HTTP is not supported.
All bodies are `application/json`. Timestamps are ISO 8601 UTC.
Energy is in kWh, power in kW, current in A, voltage in V, temperature in °C,
and SOC is an integer percentage 0–100.

Optional request signing using HMAC-SHA256 is supported for high-security
operations (see §2.2.4 of the v1.3 specification).

externalDocs.description: Tellus Power developer documentation — guides, diagnostics, runbooks, and proposed API extensions

servers.description:
Production (HOSTNAME PENDING).
The v1.3 specification uses `api.chargingplatform.com` as a placeholder; the
real production hostname has not yet been confirmed by the developer team.
Test / Sandbox (HOSTNAME PENDING). To be confirmed by the developer team.

tags.name: Operator — Authentication
tags.description: OAuth2 client-credentials grant for CPMS / EMS / Aggregator integrations.

tags.name: Operator — Sites & Devices
tags.description: Read-only resource queries — sites accessible to the authenticated client, and per-device detail with connector states.

tags.name: Operator — Remote Control
tags.description: Initiate charging, stop charging, V2G discharge, and set charging schedules remotely.

tags.name: Operator — Charging Records
tags.description: Historical session data (CDR — Charging Detail Records).

tags.name: Operator — Energy & Flexibility
tags.description: Aggregated energy time-series and load-flexibility / demand-response control.

tags.name: Operator — Telemetry Stream (WebSocket)
tags.description: Real-time telemetry subscription via WebSocket. Documented here for reference; the connection is established outside the OpenAPI request/response model.

tags.name: Charger — Provisioning
tags.description: First-time device registration and access-token issuance.

tags.name: Charger — Reporting
tags.description: Heartbeat, real-time telemetry, event reporting, charging-record upload.

tags.name: Charger — Commands
tags.description: Long-polling for pending commands and reporting their execution status.

tags.name: Operator-to-operator (legacy interop)
tags.description:
Older roaming-style interface (PascalCase fields, PUT methods) used for
data exchange between operator platforms. Modern partners should normally
integrate via the Operator-side API instead.

operatorOAuth2.description:
OAuth2 client-credentials grant for operator-side integrations.
Obtain an access token by POSTing `client_id` and `client_secret`
to `/v1/operator/oauth/token`. Tokens are valid for 24 hours
(86,400 seconds).

scopes.read: Read-only access to sites, devices, charging records, telemetry, and aggregated energy.
scopes.write: Write access — remote start, stop, discharge, schedule, flexibility commands.

deviceBearer.description:
Charger-side bearer token. Obtained by exchanging `device_id` and
`device_secret` (issued at registration) at `/v1/device/token`.
Valid for 24 hours.

ApiEnvelope.description:
Standard response envelope. `code` is `0` on success and a non-zero
application code on error (see Error Codes — §14).

code.description: 0 = success; otherwise an error code per the Error Codes section.

SiteStatusSummary.description: Counts of connectors at the site by current state.

connector_id.description: 1-based connector index, unique within a device.

power.description: Current power (kW). Negative when state is `discharging`.

energy_delivered.description: Energy delivered in the current session (kWh). 0 if no session.

soc.description: State of charge (%) of the connected vehicle, if known.

cost.description:
Session cost. Negative cost indicates revenue earned from a V2G
discharge session.

charge_energy.description: Total charging energy in the bucket (kWh).

discharge_energy.description: Total V2G discharge energy in the bucket (kWh).

max_power.description: Peak power observed during the bucket (kW).

transaction_id.description: External transaction identifier (e.g., the partner CPMS's session ID).

result.description: Optional command result (e.g., `{ energy_delivered }` after a charging command completes).

params.description:
Command-specific parameters.

- For `set_power_limit`: `{ power_limit_percent: int 0–100, duration: int seconds, reason?: string }`
- For `pause_charging`: `{ duration: int seconds, reason?: string }`

expires_in.description: Validity period of the secret (seconds). Periodic rotation is recommended.

data.description:
Event-specific payload. For example:

- `plug_in`: `{ vehicle_connected: boolean }`
- `fault`: `{ fault_code: int, fault_message: string, details: object }`
- `start` / `stop`: `{ reason: string }`

TelemetryFrame.description:
A single telemetry frame pushed by the operator-side WebSocket telemetry
stream (§13.2.7). The server emits one frame per device on a regular cadence
(typically every few seconds) for the device IDs the client subscribed to
when establishing the WebSocket connection.

LegacyMeter.description: Meter information for legacy work-status push.

post.description:
Exchange `client_id` and `client_secret` for a Bearer access token.
Tokens are valid for 24 hours (86,400 seconds).

get.description: Retrieves a list of sites accessible to the authenticated client.

Retrieves detailed information about a specific charger, including connector states.

**Related:** proposed extensions to this entity (component-level health, OCPP version metadata) are documented in [Proposed Extensions](https://developers.telluspowergroup.com/diagnostics/proposed-extensions).

externalDocs.description: Proposed component-health and OCPP-metadata extensions to this entity

post.description:
Remotely start charging on a specific connector.

**Related:** if a remote-start fails or the connector gets stuck in `preparing` state, see the *Connector stuck in Preparing state* runbook in [Diagnostic Runbooks](https://developers.telluspowergroup.com/diagnostics/runbooks).

externalDocs.description: Runbook for "Connector stuck in Preparing state" — for diagnosing failed start attempts

post.description:
Initiates vehicle-to-grid discharging on a connector. Only valid for V2G-capable hardware.

**Related:** if a discharge command is accepted but power doesn't flow, see the *V2G discharge initiated but power not flowing* runbook in [Diagnostic Runbooks](https://developers.telluspowergroup.com/diagnostics/runbooks).

externalDocs.description: Runbook for "V2G discharge initiated but power not flowing" — including ISO 15118-20 negotiation and BMS-refusal scenarios

post.description: Stores a scheduled charging plan on the connector. The charger executes the schedule autonomously, even without continuous platform connectivity.

get.description:
Retrieves historical charging records, optionally filtered by device, connector, or time range.

**Related:** when investigating fault-related `stop_reason` values, refer to the [Fault Dictionary](https://developers.telluspowergroup.com/diagnostics/fault-dictionary).

externalDocs.description: Fault dictionary — for interpreting fault-related stop_reasons in charging records

get.description: Returns aggregated energy metrics (charge / discharge / max power / avg power) over a specified time period, optionally grouped by site.

parameters.name: site_ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated site IDs to filter to.

post.description:
Sends a load-control command to a group of chargers (e.g., reduce
power by 50% across an entire site, or pause all charging for 10
minutes for grid-balancing purposes).

get.description: Retrieves the execution status of a previously issued command (e.g., from a remote-start, stop, discharge, schedule, or flexibility call).

**WebSocket endpoint.** Connect to `wss://<base>/v1/operator/stream?devices=<comma-separated device_ids>`
with the access token in the `Authorization` header during the WebSocket
handshake (or as a `?token=...` query parameter).

The server pushes JSON telemetry frames (see `TelemetryFrame` schema)
for the subscribed device IDs at the platform's configured cadence.

This endpoint cannot be tested via the standard "Try it" console — use
a WebSocket client. The schema below describes one frame the server emits.

parameters.name: devices
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of device IDs to subscribe to.

101.description:
WebSocket handshake successful. Subsequent traffic is JSON-encoded
`TelemetryFrame` messages, one per device per cadence interval.

post.description:
Called when a charger connects to the platform for the first time.
Returns `device_id` and `device_secret` which are then used to
obtain temporary access tokens via `/v1/device/token`.
Exchange `device_id` + `device_secret` for a temporary Bearer token.

Periodic (e.g., every 30 seconds) report from the charger of online
status. The response may include pending commands queued for the device.

**Related:** for diagnosing connectivity issues such as stale heartbeat or reconnect storms, see the [Diagnostic Runbooks](https://developers.telluspowergroup.com/diagnostics/runbooks).

externalDocs.description: Connectivity diagnostic runbook for stale-heartbeat and reconnect-storm scenarios

200.description: Heartbeat acknowledged. Pending commands may be returned.

post.description:
Periodic upload of real-time electrical telemetry (typically every 5
seconds during charging or discharging). Supports single-phase and
three-phase via the optional `phase_details` block.

**Related:** proposed component-level fields (contactor cycles, fan RPM, isolation tests, V2G inverter health) are described in [Proposed Extensions](https://developers.telluspowergroup.com/diagnostics/proposed-extensions).

externalDocs.description: Proposed component-level telemetry fields (contactor cycles, fan RPM, isolation tests, V2G inverter health)

post.description:
Reports device events such as plug-in, plug-out, charging start /
stop, faults, and alarms.

**Related:** for interpreting `event_type=fault` payloads, see the [Fault Dictionary](https://developers.telluspowergroup.com/diagnostics/fault-dictionary).

externalDocs.description: Fault code reference for interpreting `event_type=fault` payloads

post.description: After a charging session ends, the charger uploads the complete charging record (CDR).

get.description:
The charger polls for pending commands using long polling. If no
command is available, the request hangs until the timeout expires
(default 30 seconds, max 60) or a command arrives.

**Related:** proposed remote diagnostic command types (`reboot`, `run_self_test`, `calibrate_sensor`, `fetch_logs`) are documented in [Proposed Extensions](https://developers.telluspowergroup.com/diagnostics/proposed-extensions).

externalDocs.description: Proposed remote diagnostic command types (reboot, run_self_test, calibrate_sensor, fetch_logs, etc.)

post.description: Reports the execution result of a previously received command.
The charger reports firmware-upgrade progress or final result.

put.description:
Legacy interface (§3 of the v1.3 spec). Implemented by the charging
operator and called by a peer "data-demander" platform.
PascalCase request and response fields, PUT method.

LastQueryTime.description: Last query time, format `yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss`. Empty queries all.

put.description:
Legacy push interface (§4). Implemented by the data-demander platform
and called by the charging operator when device status changes.
Legacy interface (§5). Batch query of real-time status for up to 50 devices.

post.description:
Legacy interface (§6). Implemented by the device operator and called by
the customer platform. Response time SHOULD NOT exceed 3 seconds.

Legacy interface (§7). Implemented by the customer platform and called
by the device operator after a charge starts or fails. Retry policy:
5 attempts at intervals of 1m, 5m, 60m, 180m, 24h. After exhaustion,
the customer can call `query_station_status` to fetch state.
Legacy interface (§8). Response time SHOULD NOT exceed 1 second.
Legacy V2G start interface (§9). Response time SHOULD NOT exceed 3 seconds.

x-error-codes.suggested_action: Refresh or obtain a new token.

charger_side.expected_cadence: Telemetry every 5 seconds; heartbeat every 30 seconds.

x-rate-limits.exceeded: Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 with `code: 5001`.
